name: Milk (Other mammals) (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh whole goat milk (representative non-bovine mammalian milk)

components.name: water
components.source: USDA SR Legacy goat milk (NDB 01106) via nutritionvalue.org 11116000

components.name: lactose
components.source: USDA SR Legacy goat milk (NDB 01106); total sugars 4.45-4.5 g/100g are essentially all lactose
components.evidence: DOMINANT carbon/energy source; galactose+glucose disaccharide, MW 342.3. Total sugars in goat milk = lactose (no sucrose).

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: Milk free-sugar literature (free glucose in milk ~10-70 mg/L)
components.evidence: Trace free monosaccharide from lactose turnover; not a major pool.

components.name: D-galactose
components.source: Milk free-sugar literature (free galactose in milk trace)
components.evidence: Trace free monosaccharide; liberated by lactose hydrolysis in vivo.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Supplee & Bellis J Biol Chem (citric acid content of milk ~0.172%); goat milk citrate slightly lower ~0.13-0.16% (J Dairy Sci S0022-0302(06)72227-5)
components.evidence: DOMINANT organic acid in milk; ~90% soluble citrate salts of Ca/Mg/K, key buffer. MW 192.12. Was MISSING from the FooDB profile.

components.name: orotic acid
components.source: Agrarforschung Schweiz 2008, orotic acid in cow/ewe/goat milk (goat 1.08-2.48 mg/100g, mean ~1.5)
components.evidence: Pyrimidine-pathway intermediate characteristic of milk; MW 156.1. Was MISSING.

components.name: lactic acid
components.source: Dairy chemistry (fresh unfermented milk lactic acid <0.02 g/100g)
components.evidence: Only trace in FRESH milk; rises sharply only on fermentation. Included at low value with note.

components.name: acetic acid
components.evidence: Trace in fresh milk; product of lactose degradation, unquantified here.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: ~80% casein, ~20% whey; the amino-acid amounts below are the protein-derived profile.

components.name: L-tryptophan
components.source: USDA SR goat milk via foodfact.info
components.evidence: Protein-bound residue; mM shown as potential on full hydrolysis (MW 204.23).

components.name: L-threonine
components.source: USDA SR goat milk via foodfact.info

components.name: L-isoleucine
components.source: USDA SR goat milk via foodfact.info

components.name: L-leucine
components.source: USDA SR goat milk via foodfact.info

components.name: L-lysine
components.source: USDA SR goat milk via foodfact.info

components.name: L-methionine
components.source: USDA SR goat milk via foodfact.info

components.name: L-cystine
components.source: USDA SR goat milk via foodfact.info

components.name: L-phenylalanine
components.source: USDA SR goat milk via foodfact.info

components.name: L-tyrosine
components.source: USDA SR goat milk via foodfact.info

components.name: L-valine
components.source: USDA SR goat milk via foodfact.info

components.name: L-arginine
components.source: USDA SR goat milk via foodfact.info

components.name: L-histidine
components.source: USDA SR goat milk via foodfact.info

components.name: L-alanine
components.source: USDA SR goat milk via foodfact.info

components.name: L-aspartate
components.source: USDA SR goat milk via foodfact.info

components.name: L-glutamate
components.source: USDA SR goat milk via foodfact.info
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid (from casein glutamine/glutamate). MW 147.13.

components.name: glycine
components.source: USDA SR goat milk via foodfact.info

components.name: L-proline
components.source: USDA SR goat milk via foodfact.info

components.name: L-serine
components.source: USDA SR goat milk via foodfact.info

components.name: taurine
components.evidence: Free amino sulfonic acid, notable in mammalian milk. MW 125.15.

components.name: pantothenic acid (B5)
components.source: USDA SR Legacy goat milk (breed range 0.19-0.31)

components.name: folate (B9)
components.source: USDA SR Legacy goat milk (folate DFE 1 ug)
components.evidence: Goat milk is characteristically LOW in folate.

components.name: cyanocobalamin (B12)
components.evidence: B12; goat milk lower than cow. Cobalt is supplied nutritionally as B12.

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.evidence: Present but not in USDA panel; approximate.

components.name: retinol (vitamin A)
components.source: USDA SR Legacy goat milk (RAE 57 ug, retinol 56 ug)
components.evidence: Goat milk vitamin A is essentially all preformed retinol (goats convert carotene to retinol).

components.name: vitamin D
components.evidence: Variable; native goat-milk D is low, this value may include fortification.

components.name: calcium
components.source: USDA SR Legacy goat milk / milkfacts.info
components.evidence: Largely as colloidal calcium phosphate + citrate; MW 40.08.

components.name: phosphate (phosphorus)
components.source: USDA SR Legacy goat milk / milkfacts.info
components.evidence: As P; inorganic + organic phosphate. MW(P) 30.97.

components.name: potassium
components.source: USDA SR Legacy goat milk / milkfacts.info

components.name: magnesium
components.source: USDA SR Legacy goat milk / milkfacts.info

components.name: zinc
components.source: USDA SR Legacy goat milk / milkfacts.info

components.name: molybdate (molybdenum)
components.evidence: Genuine ultratrace essential element; unquantified, kept.

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: Milk fat; fatty acids below. Note goat fat is rich in medium-chain C6-C10 (caproic/caprylic/capric).

components.name: cholesterol
components.evidence: KEPT: legitimate animal-food sterol (this is milk, not a plant food).

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: non-nutritive ultratrace contaminant, not a defined milk growth nutrient

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: free cobalt ion is not a milk nutrient pool; cobalt is supplied nutritionally as vitamin B12 (already listed)

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: a furanocoumarin from Apiaceae plants; biologically implausible in mammalian milk; FooDB predicted/contaminant

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: a microbial-fermentation food preservative additive, not a natural constituent of milk

remove.name: (E)-2-Phenyl-2-butenal
remove.reason: trace Maillard/flavor volatile aldehyde, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: non-physiological stereoisomer/DB artifact; the real nutrient is L-isoleucine (already listed)

missing_added: citric acid (dominant organic acid in milk, ~0.15 g/100g; was absent)
lactic acid + acetic acid (trace in fresh milk, flagged)
lactose quantified (4.5 g/100g, the dominant carbon source)
chloride, choline, niacin, pantothenic acid, folate, vitamin E, vitamin K, biotin amounts
keep_uncovered: 24,25-dihydroxyvitamin D3 (genuine trace vitamin D catabolite; no BiGG met)
milk oligosaccharides (genuine, prebiotic; no single BiGG met)
orotic acid (kept in components; may lack a BiGG exchange met)
sources: USDA SR Legacy 'Goat's milk, whole' NDB 01106 (via nutritionvalue.org 11116000 and foodfact.info amino-acid table)
MilkFacts.info Nutrient Content of Milk Varieties (cow/goat/sheep comparison)
Supplee & Bellis, 'Citric acid content of milk and milk products', J Biol Chem (milk citrate ~0.172%)
Garnsworthy et al., 'Variation of Milk Citrate with Stage of Lactation', J Dairy Sci S0022-0302(06)72227-5
Agrarforschung Schweiz 2008, orotic acid in cow/ewe/goat milk
FooDB FOOD00690 (Milk, other mammals) source record
notes: Curated as whole goat milk, the representative non-bovine mammalian milk. DOMINANT carbon source is LACTOSE (~4.5 g/100g, ~131 mM); the principal organic acid is CITRATE (~0.15 g/100g, ~7.8 mM, was missing and is a key Ca/Mg buffer + carbon source), with trace orotic/lactic/acetic acids. Casein-rich protein (3.56 g) supplies a full amino-acid pool (Glu/Pro/Leu highest). Distinctive goat-milk signature is abundant medium-chain fatty acids (C6:0-C10:0). Removed plant furanocoumarin, a preservative additive, a stereoisomer artifact, a flavor volatile, and non-nutritive trace ions (Ni, free Co2+). Cholesterol KEPT (legitimate animal food).
